_header.file_role: E1 training-side T2 anchor AC baseline config: Llama-3.1-405B (hand-written, wave 2)

metadata.source_note: Meta Llama-3.1-405B (base, BF16): 126 dense decoder layers, d_model 16384, GQA (128 Q heads / 8 KV heads, d_head 128), SwiGLU FFN 53248, vocab 128256, RoPE base 500000, RMSNorm eps 1e-5, untied embeddings. Architecture facts: meta-llama/llama-models models/sku_list.py llama3_1_405b arch_args (dim/n_layers/n_heads/n_kv_heads/vocab/rope_theta/norm_eps, ffn_dim_multiplier 1.2 + multiple_of 4096); FFN 53248 derived via the official formula in models/llama3/model.py (4*16384 -> *2/3 -> *1.2 -> round up to 4096) and cross-checked against the Llama 3 herd paper (arXiv 2407.21783v3, Table 3: 126 layers / 16384 dim / 53248 FFN / 128 heads / 8 KV heads). parallelism block mirrors the paper's Table 4 stage-1 pretraining layout as closely as AC schema allows: TP=8, CP=1, DP=64 from Table 4 exactly; PP=14 instead of the paper's PP=16 because AC requires pipeline_parallel to divide n_layers (126 = 14 x 9) while Meta's real PP=16 uses a non-uniform stage split that schema 0.3 cannot represent (recorded as an E1 deviation; PP=14 is the nearest divisor to 16). Unrepresented in schema 0.3: llama3-type RoPE scaling (8k -> 128k) encoded as none + original_max_position 8192. params counts follow AC's parameter ledger (2*vocab*d embeddings + 2*d_model RMSNorm per layer); for dense models the ledger books active == total.
